2604.21698.title: Fixation Sequences as Time Series: A Topological Approach to Dyslexia Detection
2604.21698.paper_summary: Persistent homology, a method from topological data analysis, extracts robust, multi-scale features from data. It produces stable representations of time series by applying varying thresholds to their values (a process known as a \textit{filtration}). We develop novel filtrations for time series and introduce topological methods for the analysis of eye-tracking data, by interpreting fixation sequences as time series, and constructing ``hybrid models'' that combine topological features with traditional statistical features. We empirically evaluate our method by applying it to the task of dyslexia detection from eye-tracking-while-reading data using the Copenhagen Corpus, which contains scanpaths from dyslexic and non-dyslexic L1 and L2 readers. Our hybrid models outperform existing approaches that rely solely on traditional features, showing that persistent homology captures complementary information encoded in fixation sequences. The strength of these topological features is further underscored by their achieving performance comparable to established baseline methods. Importantly, our proposed filtrations outperform existing ones.
2604.21698.paper_summary_zh: 持續同調是一種來自拓撲數據分析的方法，它從數據中提取穩健的多尺度特徵。<lb/>它通過對時間序列的值應用不同的閾值（這一過程稱為\textit{過濾}）來生成穩定的時間序列表示。<lb/>我們為時間序列開發了新穎的過濾方法，並通過將注視序列解釋為時間序列，引入拓撲方法來分析眼動追蹤數據，並構建“混合模型”，將拓撲特徵與傳統統計特徵相結合。<lb/>我們通過將其應用於使用哥本哈根語料庫的眼動追蹤閱讀數據進行的閱讀障礙檢測任務來實證評估我們的方法，該語料庫包含了閱讀障礙和非閱讀障礙的L1和L2讀者的掃描路徑。<lb/>我們的混合模型在性能上超越了僅依賴傳統特徵的現有方法，顯示出持續同調捕捉到的注視序列中編碼的互補信息。<lb/>這些拓撲特徵的強度進一步通過其達到與既定基準方法相當的性能來強調。<lb/>重要的是，我們提出的過濾方法優於現有的過濾方法。
2604.21698.author: Marius Huber et.al.
2604.21698.authors: Marius Huber, David R. Reich, Lena A. Jäger
